curation_notes: Re-curated with updated hint guidelines. The primary vulnerability is in LoginUIExtension.vm where $request.identityOAuth (a user-controlled GET parameter) flows into $statusMsg and is rendered unescaped in an HTML comment (<!-- ======= ${statusMsg} ====== -->). The fix adds $escapetool.xml($statusMsg). Secondary locations are the t and tp Velocity macros in IdentityOAuthConfigMacros.vm which output translation strings without escaping; the fix wraps output in $escapetool.xml() or $escapetool.javascript() depending on context. WebHome.xml also received escaping fixes but was excluded from locations since it only renders static translation keys. L1 broadened to cover the full UI template layer (~20 files). L3 narrowed to the login page extension and translation-rendering macros (~3-5 files). Pre-assigned vuln_class xss confirmed by CWE-79, advisory description, and patch diff.
